summary:
The key pathological feature in Eisenmenger syndrome is central pulmonary artery dilatation resulting from chronic pressure overload and vessel remodeling. Right ventricle wall thickening persists due to ongoing hemodynamic stress, making reversal of RV/LV enlargement inaccurate.

The summary must be concise—only two sentences—and focus exclusively on clinically significant findings directly related to the condition being asked about.

Only include information present within Agent1's own reasoning—the summary should reflect only what they concluded based solely on their analysis.

Do NOT add external knowledge beyond what was used by them during evaluation.

Okay, let me now create an accurate one-sentence summary reflecting exactly what Agent1 reasoned:

Central pulmonary artery dilatation is a well-documented hallmark pathology in Eisenmenger syndrome caused by chronic pressure overload and vascular remodeling.  
Right ventricular wall thickness increases progressively and does not return to normal despite reverse shunting, contradicting any claim of normalization.
